notes: One entry per distinct existing dev/docs/* (or in-tree README) doc that the Phase 1 audit touched, either via a subsystem survey (e*/g*/a*/t*) with a Drift table, or via a docs-existing summary (d01-d09) with Preservable/MAY BE STALE blocks. Per user feedback, dev/docs/ files are read-only historical reference; 'migrate-target' and 'rewrite' dispositions both mean the Phase 2 writer produces a NEW module doc and leaves the old doc as historical pointer. Entries cite the evidence files Phase 2 should consult for preservable signal.

entries.rationale: Survey reports 8 drift rows, only 3 break-severity, all narrow (registerTick name/no-priority, deltaMs vs seconds, installRenderLoop unclear). The doc is a foundational architecture overview — five rules, three-tier model, opinions-NOT-held, GMT-as-senior-dependency framing — most of which is aspirational/decisional rather than API-current. The drift is localized to the render-loop section. New tick-registry module doc captures current API; old doc preserved as the architecture-rationale anchor.
entries.preservable_signal: Five design principles + rationale; three-tier model; 'opinions we're NOT holding' list; GMT-as-senior-dependency framing; rejection rationale for minimal core and opinionated engine; aspiration that GMT eventually ports as a plugin (d01 summary lines 38-46).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 21 drift rows with 8 break-severity covering the primary public API (no `defineFeature` export, no `enum`/`string` param types, `bool` vs `boolean` mismatch, no lifecycle hooks/ctx, no `cacheKey` field, no `useFeatureParam`, no `ui` field, `tabConfig` shape changed). Major aspirational gap — the doc describes a target API that doesn't ship. Per Phase 2 user direction, leave old doc as historical/aspirational reference and write new module doc against actual `FeatureSystem.ts` + `createFeatureSlice.ts` + `defineEnumParam.ts` + `typedSlices.ts`.
entries.preservable_signal: 'One file per feature' aspiration; six auto-derivations enumerated; GMT audit findings that motivated strict enforcement (silent setter-name mismatch, per-leaf-subscription performance cliff, lint catching only 60% of isolation cases); per-stop gradient as future direction (d01 summary lines 48-57).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; only summarised in d01. Captures the three-file plugin pattern, boot timeline (t=0..t=∞), and failure-mode table — heavy on decisional/aspirational content (dev-vs-prod severity philosophy, idempotency semantics, opt-out semantics). Doc 21 (Code Review) noted engine-gmt is a coupled domain layer NOT a clean plugin — this contract needs an engine-gmt coupling clarification but its core philosophy holds. New module doc on plugin contract should link back here for design rationale.
entries.preservable_signal: Boot timeline with explicit t=0..t=∞ stages; failure-mode table mapping each violation to its dev/prod behaviour; dev-vs-prod severity philosophy; aspiration that GMT itself ports as an 'apex reference' plugin (d01 summary lines 59-67).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 13 drift rows, MOST break-severity, including fictional APIs (`installSceneIO({fields, registerField})`, `<SaveMenu/>`, `<LoadDropZone/>`, Ctrl+S/O bindings), entire undocumented subsystems (@engine/menu, @engine/help, @engine/hud, @engine/pwa-update, @engine/render-dialog, BucketRender, PauseControls), and reversed closed decisions (help-browser + PWA-update were declared app-specific but shipped as engine plugins). Survey explicit recommendation: REWRITE. Doc 21 'plugin pattern not uniform' note also lands here.
entries.preservable_signal: Camera adapter rationale (the three different camera shapes); screenshot-folding story as canonical 'no duplicate UX' example; rejected alternatives (auto-install everything, append-only slot registration); hotkey-choice rationale; `installModulation` → `installAnimation` rename candidate (d01 summary lines 69-79).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 16 drift rows, most break-severity. Doc describes aspirational opt-in-context primitive library (5 contexts: AnimationContext, UndoContext, ContextMenuContext, ShortcutContext, FeatureCompileContext) + `useFeatureParam` hook that don't exist. Actual code has single `StoreCallbacksContext` + direct-store imports in Slider/Knob/Vector*/Dock/AutoFeaturePanel. ColorPicker, TabBar, ContextMenu primitives named in doc don't match shipped components. Survey explicit recommendation: REWRITE. Doc will mislead anyone writing a new primitive.
entries.preservable_signal: Opt-in context pattern itself as a target design; AdvancedGradientEditor litmus-test framing; OKLab rationale from GMT retrospective ('colors washing out' until OKLab adopted); audit-gate aspiration ('before 1.0, grep every primitive for useEngineStore'); long-term Tailwind→CSS-variables direction (d01 summary lines 81-90).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 10 drift rows, mostly minor/info severity. One BREAK row is an internal-rationale inconsistency about who wins tie-breaks (doc says first-registered wins; Shortcuts.ts comment says last-registered wins) — affects narrative only since the priority-10 override makes the question moot. Survey explicit note: 'No Recommendation line needed — drift is small and contained.' The 2026-04-30 per-scope-stack revert is documented in the doc and matches code. Doc is among the most reconciled engine docs; new module doc captures shipped surface; old doc stays as supplementary reference.
entries.preservable_signal: Full unified-vs-per-scope migration story (the two structural failure modes); 'Ctrl+Z popped a camera move when the user meant to undo a slider tweak' bug class; rejected 'keep one stack, default unscoped to skip camera' alternative; F2b deferral rationale (d01 summary lines 92-102).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit (e08 covers Undo + Shortcuts plugin but its drift table is rooted at doc 06). Per d01 summary, doc describes pluggable shortcut registry with Mod abstraction, scope stack, dev tooling — substantial aspirational content (`shortcuts.trace`/`lookup`, `smoke:audit-shortcuts` test, rebinding UI). Implementation status unclear from summary alone but flagged as 'whether registry fully implemented vs partially'. Best treated as design reference + canonical shortcut tables; new module doc on shortcuts plugin (if produced) points back.
entries.preservable_signal: Audit findings that motivated the registry (one big switch statement, can't disable during modal, can't rebind without fork, ad-hoc isTimelineHovered threading); scope-beats-priority rationale with timeline-hover Ctrl+Z example; range-inputs-not-ignored as preserved GMT good UX; rebinding-UI aspiration (d01 summary lines 104-113).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 17 drift rows but explicit recommendation is MINOR-EDITS. Two BREAK rows (`track.type !== 'float'` silently drops non-float tracks while doc promises step interpolation for bool/enum/string/image; log/Bezier interaction). Most rows are info/warn pointing at specific corrections (import paths, missing audio-clip-sync/scrub-hook coverage, missing render-popup section). Survey notes 'architecture sections of the doc are largely accurate; type signatures, public-API examples, and the binder-resolution table need targeted updates rather than a rewrite.' Per-scope stacks already captured in doc. Per user feedback dev/docs are read-only, so new module doc captures current shipped surface; old doc stays as supplementary reference.
entries.preservable_signal: Full rationale for log-value-space interpolation (linear lerp on julia.zoom 1→1e-30 collapses 99.999% of timeline); camera-pair linear-in-zoom math justification; DD-precision two-sum/Veltkamp-split rationale; Maya/Blender tangent-mode convention adoption; de Casteljau split rationale; FPS Keep/Match (DCC parity); deterministic playback motivation; F5/F6/F7/F12/F13 fragility lineage (d01 summary lines 115-133).

entries.rationale: Not covered by any subsystem audit; only summarised in d01. d01 flags this as 'one of the most aspirational engine docs — derive/bridge mechanisms likely partially or not built'. Tripartite coordination taxonomy (`dependsOn`/`derive`/`bridge`) is design rationale that explains how engine intends to escape GMT's ad-hoc patterns. Preserve as design reference; F7 fragility-audit entry already verified the cycle was imaginary, so the migration urgency dropped.
entries.preservable_signal: Tripartite coordination taxonomy as engine's stated alternative to GMT's ad-hoc patterns; F7 (animationStore↔fractalStore presumed cycle) as the canonical problem this design solves; two-mechanisms rationale; onChange-default flip; migration aspirations (AnimationSystem.tsx, EngineBridge.tsx, light-auto-focus) (d01 summary lines 135-144).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 14+ drift rows with at least 8 break-severity covering primary public APIs (no `useViewport()`, no `viewport.setMode/setFixedResolution/setAdaptive` imperatives, no `viewport.onResize/onQualityChange/onModeChange` subscriptions, no `<ViewportArea canvasSlot>`, no `<PerformanceWarning>`+suggestion registry, no `viewport.*` namespace, no Custom mode, no environment plugin). Survey explicit recommendation: REWRITE. A few items match (algorithm unification, hold semantics, badge component). Doc describes a planned design that the code does not implement.
entries.preservable_signal: The 80/20 framing (size modes/DPR/adaptive shared; what 'reduce quality' means is app-specific); full audit-table classification methodology (GENERIC/SEMI-GENERIC/fractal-specific/app-specific); GMT vs toy-fluid duplication story as motivation; rejected alternatives (qualityFraction as enum, plugin-creates-canvas, plugin bakes mobile detection); 6-step extraction sequence; future `@engine/environment` plugin scope (d01 summary lines 146-158).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d02 summary captures it. Doc 21 (Code Review) explicitly contradicts the 'uniform contract' framing — shipped plugins do NOT all follow the module-scope-registry + _rev + useSyncExternalStore + idempotent installX pattern (Camera/Undo/Viewport/Shortcuts use other shapes; survey e07 confirms each plugin invented its own contract). Doc needs reframing as 'slot-host plugin recipe' rather than 'universal plugin contract' but the seven rules + idempotency requirement + reference table remain useful for the slot-host subset. Preserve as design reference; cross-link from plugins-host module doc.
entries.preservable_signal: Seven plugin rules (R2 ties to F1 frozen-registry trap); idempotency requirement (two bundles must converge); `when: () => boolean` predicate over conditional registration; dev-only `window.__myThing` expose pattern; reference plugins table (TopBar/Menu/Hud/Help/Shortcuts) (d02 summary lines 37-56).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit (e06 covers Adaptive Resolution but is rooted at doc 10). d02 summary explicitly flags 'Nothing stale — recent (post-2026-04-26 fix referenced inline) and detailed.' Unified TSAA+Adaptive Resolution protocol with AccumulationController/AdaptiveResolution split. Treat as canonical accumulating-renderer reference; touch only if Phase 2 wiring discovers drift.
entries.preservable_signal: Architectural split between protocol (AccumulationController) and algorithm (AdaptiveResolution-pure); initial-state push pattern for async-bootable renderers; deep-accumulation protection (accumThreshold = floor(sampleCap * 0.5)); fluid-toy two-scale decoupling; plumbing-pitfalls audit checklist; 5-step recipe for authoring a new accumulating renderer (d02 summary lines 60-81).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d02 summary explicitly flags 'Nothing stale.' Pattern doc for `defineAppHandles<T>` with usage matrix, dev-only globalThis expose, decision rationale for narrow handles vs grab-bag. Doc is current and useful — keep as-is.
entries.preservable_signal: Full decision matrix (when to use vs alternatives); useSnapshot semantics for high-frequency writes; rejection of grab-bag pattern; smoke-test access shape (d02 summary lines 85-105).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d02 summary explicitly flags 'Nothing stale.' Cookbook with TSAA + pause-button worked example, three-question triage (STATE/BEHAVIOUR/UI), tree-placement table, 10-minute rule, 8-step checklist. Doc is current and useful — keep as-is.
entries.preservable_signal: Three triage questions; danger signs that mean a feature is NOT generic; tree placement table; rule of thumb for what stays in engine-gmt; 10-minute rule (d02 summary lines 109-127).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 6 drift rows, NO break-severity. All warn/info. GMT's actual panels.ts uses `items: [...]` exclusively and never invokes the `features:` shorthand or `widgets: {before, after, between}` documented forms. Doc's spec is broader than GMT's adoption — primarily a gap, not contradictions. Doc is structurally correct; new app-panels module doc captures current GMT manifest pattern; old doc stays as full PanelDefinition contract reference.
entries.preservable_signal: Full PanelItem union types (9 types — feature, widget, section, separator, collapsible, accordion, compilable, compile-dropdown, runtime-section); compile-spinner contract (scheduler emits, NOT UI components — load-bearing); when-to-use items vs features shorthand decision list; GMT's 10-panel manifest example; migration deletions list (d02 summary lines 131-150).

entries.rationale: Doc is explicitly self-marked 'Status: Research. No code changes yet — proposes the shape, next session implements.' Code DID ship: createStateLibrarySlice.ts, installStateLibrary.ts, viewLibrary.ts, StateLibraryPanel, StateLibraryToast all live; substantial divergence from proposed shape (two keys arrayKey+activeIdKey not single storeKey, async returning Promise<string>, explicit action-names map). Survey explicit recommendation: 'Mark doc 15 as historical research/proposal note in its status header, and create a new shipped-reference doc.' Doc captures load-bearing design rationale (four-block split, capture/apply callback shape, multiple-libraries-per-app insight, composition-overlays separation) that the new module doc should link back to.
entries.preservable_signal: Four-block split of CameraManagerPanel.tsx as analysis template; capture/apply callback shape; split of GMT-specific bits from generic bits; multiple-libraries-per-app design ('Views' + 'Color Palettes' + 'Brush Presets'); 'fluid-toy's snapshot is a fractal-config-plus-camera blob, NOT a camera' insight that drove StateLibrary naming; composition overlays separation as a design rule; shortcuts already generic (d02 summary lines 154-179).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d02 summary explicitly flags 'Nothing stale.' Reference doc for declaration-merging engine store with app-specific DDFS slice keys + installStateLibrary dynamic keys. Two-target rule (AppFeatureSlices + FeatureStateMap), SliceFromParams helper, canonical pattern. Doc is current and useful — keep as-is.
entries.preservable_signal: Two-target table (AppFeatureSlices vs FeatureStateMap) — load-bearing rule; full storeTypes.ts canonical pattern; state-library augmentation pattern with literal-key duplication; rejected `declareStateLibrary` helper rationale; reference implementations (d02 summary lines 183-203).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 10 drift rows, 4 break-severity but the breaks are narrow (3-flag return shape; `100vh` vs `100dvh`/`100svh`; `MobileScrollIntro` primitive entirely missing from doc; adoption-checklist step 1 ordering). Survey explicit recommendation: 'minor-edits + add MobileScrollIntro section. Core architecture is correctly described.' Doc was self-marked 🚧 incomplete. New module doc captures shipped surface; old doc stays as supplementary reference.
entries.preservable_signal: Sibling-app adoption checklist (6 steps); topbar item culling via when:() => !isMobileSnapshot(); iOS sticky-positioning + safe-area-inset trick; touch-input cede rule (e.pointerType === 'touch' early-return to drei native); mobileMenu API surface design pattern; future `@engine/environment` plugin scope (d02 summary lines 207-231).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d02 summary explicitly flags 'Nothing stale — doc explicitly tracks status of every item.' Ground-truth doc for F1-F18 fragility findings with priority order + audit methodology. Living tracker; updated 2026-04-30 with F2b second-pass revert; cadence is 'before each major version bump, or after every third core plugin lands.' Preserve as-is — Phase 2 should leave this doc alone (it's the right shape for the role it plays).
entries.preservable_signal: ALL F1-F18 findings (the doc IS the preservable signal); fix-priority order summary; F14 audit checklist (run npm run orphans, diff every file in engine-gmt/engine/** against engine/**, grep for module-level let _foo); intentional-orphan registry three buckets (d02 summary lines 240-281).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d02 summary notes this is the original audit-of-record vs doc 20 as running status tracker. d02 summary explicitly says 'Keep both in Phase 2 — 21 is the historical what-an-outside-survey-saw-at-this-point-in-time snapshot, useful for re-audit cadence calibration.' Most B-findings folded into F16/F17/F18; preserve as historical snapshot.
entries.preservable_signal: Accurate-picture-of-current-state table; plugin-pattern-not-uniform table with per-plugin status; full F14 dual-tree diff table; engine-gmt coupling acknowledgment (35+ files import engineStore directly); onboarding gaps (README stale, port 3400 vs documented 5173, demo/README.md omits registerFeatures.ts, 18 of 28 smoke tests have no npm run script, package.json `name: gmt-fractal`, express in wrong section) (d02 summary lines 287-365).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 12 drift rows with multiple break-severity covering core API changes (update→compute/render split; accumulation controlled by pipeline.setHold not binary; HISTOGRAM_REQUEST/RESULT renamed to HISTOGRAM_READBACK; ~15 worker protocol messages entirely unmentioned; two-stage compile / keepCurrent / Firefox parallel-compile disablement / generation counter / offset-guarded sync / pending-offset-sync atomic-absorb / restart Firefox cancel / spinner messaging / HDR vs LDR env-map / LinearSRGBColorSpace / deferred INIT+BOOT / setConvergenceNeeded ALL implemented but undocumented). Survey explicit recommendation: REWRITE. Doc predates the engine extraction. >10 rows, most of §3/§4 needs rewrite.
entries.preservable_signal: Full ShaderBuilder hook table (18 hooks × position × scope); requestShading + addShadingLogic deferred-generation pattern; satellite feature pattern (LightSpheres dependsOn lighting); panelConfig + CompilableFeatureSection two-level control; three-layer scalability pipeline (Authored / Tier Overrides / Hardware Caps); boot hydration sync main.tsx URL parse before ReactDOM.render; compileGate.queue routing; worker-mode gotchas catalog; atomic orbit-absorb pattern; silent absorb pattern; OrbitControls target reset rationale (d03 summary lines 17-43). Survey explicit note: '§1 (Engine-Bridge), §2 (DDFS), §5 (TickRegistry), §6 (Coordinate System), §7 (State) can stay as-is — narrative product overview.'

entries.rationale: Survey reports 16 drift rows but MOST are 'OK' or 'Missing' (gaps) rather than contradictions. The 8 OK rows confirm rendering math/algorithms remain accurate. The 8 Missing rows note the doc never enumerated the ShaderBuilder API surface or ConfigManager/UniformManager internals — those need a NEW doc rather than fixing the existing one. Survey explicit recommendation: 'Recommendation is to document, not to fix code-side drift.' Existing doc is heavy on hard-won quirks (Schlick formulas, Volumetric MIS, PT bounce bias, warp coherence) that remain canonical reference.
entries.preservable_signal: Two-Schlick-formula rationale (per-light vs reflection throughput); fog color pre-linearization rationale; lean bounce tracer (traceSceneLean) emitted alongside traceScene for PT bounces; bounce bias biasEps = pixelSizeScale * length(p_ray); volumetric stochastic gate exp2(-7+4*uVolQuality); warp coherence load-bearing (small mixing constants 7.43/1.0 keep warps coherent); two-stage compile spec; Henyey-Greenstein phase function; Beer-Lambert; sphere-area-light MIS Veach 1995 power-heuristic (β=2); stochasticSeed must have per-pixel variation via blue noise (mandatory); PT bounces don't accumulate vol (design); bench harness flag list; three-layer bucket render lock; async convergence via gl.fenceSync; tile bloom/CA seam handling (d03 summary lines 45-87).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 13 drift rows, all 'None' (matches), except one minor 'BFS vs DFS' wording inaccuracy and one optional packer-side overflow note. Survey explicit summary: 'Net: doc is highly accurate; only the BFS vs DFS wording is mildly inaccurate.' The two audited files (GraphCompiler.ts + graphAlg.ts) match doc verbatim including empty-graph fallback, v_start_d=1000.0, condition wrapping, getParam binding rules, output wiring, structural diff. New module doc captures current state; old doc stays as the canonical Modular System reference essentially unchanged.
entries.preservable_signal: Full node registry with GLSL semantics; conditional execution wraps node's GLSL in if((i-(i/cmod)*cmod)==crem); clientToFlow helper React Flow v11 gotcha; skipNextOnNodesDelete ref pattern; ghost-insert dropdown setTimeout(0) defer; Tab zoom/pan persistence via module-level persistedViewport; persistence shape (graph + pipeline); seven named preset bundles (d03 summary lines 89-115).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit (e03 audits engine-side animation, not GMT-side). d03 summary lists this doc with file paths, store action names, and timeline component paths likely shifted post-extraction. Engine doc 08_Animation.md already cross-refs this as 'app-specific implementation.' Animation-refactor doc set (d05-d07) overlays the present-day understanding. Preserve as historical scope-split reference; new engine-gmt animation doc (if created) points back.
entries.preservable_signal: Bezier solver Newton-Raphson with 4 iterations; setTangents('Unified'|'Aligned') averaging logic; scaleHandles proportional scale; post-keyframe behaviors (Hold/Loop/PingPong/Continue/OffsetLoop); soft selection types (Linear/Dome/Pinpoint/S-Curve); TickRegistry ANIMATE phase order; overriddenTracks set (modulation/audio drives skips track); legacy lights.N.prop.axis auto-remap; track grouping centralized in utils/groupTracks.ts; useGraphInteraction.ts aspirational split (d03 summary lines 117-155).

entries.rationale: Survey reports 7 drift rows, no explicit recommendation but issues are localized: file paths shifted (utils/FormulaFormat.ts → engine-gmt/utils/FormulaFormat.ts), loadGMFScene scope narrowed (no longer registers formula or applies state — caller responsibility), PNG metadata key changed (FractalData → SceneData with legacy fallback), shaderMeta subfield missing from schema, Modular special case + unknown-formula JSON fallback + SceneIO plugin layer all undocumented. Architecture intact; doc updates targeted. New module doc captures current SceneIO + GMF + PNG flow; old doc stays as supplementary export-format reference.
entries.preservable_signal: GMF-as-primary-format rationale; full Firefox VideoEncoder quirks catalog (chunk.timestamp PTS reconstruct, chunk.duration hardcode to 1/fps, bitrateMode 'constant', latencyMode 'quality'); Firefox H264 31Mbps Cisco OpenH264 Level 4.0 ceiling; WebCodecs align=2 not 16; H264Converter REMOVED rationale; FileSystemWritableFileStream not transferable; file naming patterns table; VDB Tree_float_5_4_3 grid spec; bucket render convergence threshold meaning (0.1%/0.25%/0.5%/1.0%) (d03 summary lines 157-186).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d03 summary describes this as 'institutional memory of hard-won WebGL gotchas. Every section is a Preservable bullet.' Stale items are limited to file paths post-extraction. Phase 2 should leave this doc largely alone — its value is the accumulated quirk catalog, not the file-path indices.
entries.preservable_signal: Every gotcha is preservable — TDR shader complexity, WebGL render target leak post-export, Firefox VideoEncoder fps mismatch, Firefox bitrateMode under-run, Firefox H264 31Mbps cap, WebCodecs align=2, mediabunny native AVCC, mic HTTPS requirement, Z-fighting/banding/DOF picking/DOF screen-shaking causes, iOS HalfFloat16 black screen, mobile light gizmo offset getBoundingClientRect fix, mobile panel layout matchMedia, initial canvas low-res TWO causes, FPS GC pressure fix, bucket render black stripes scissor fix, canvasPixelSize read pitfall, bucket render corruption three-layer lock, Firefox ~50% lower FPS Bugzilla refs, HalfFloat16 readback Uint16Array, env sky load default fix, sample cap startup race fix, convergence stuck causes (d03 summary lines 194-231).

entries.rationale: Not covered by a subsystem audit; d03 summary describes as 'Technical debt tracker — completed refactors, any-type triage (5 categories, 452→320), ts-ignore audit, file complexity, error handling, camera state audit, store initialization races, shader magic numbers.' Phase 2 should preserve for the institutional knowledge bullets and use as a checklist input — many items may have flipped status since.
entries.preservable_signal: any-type 5-category triage (C1 browser API gaps leave, C2 fixed, C3 structural DDFS deferred, C4 leave, C5 mediabunny leave); FeatureStateMap aspiration; file complexity table; boot timing heuristic 50ms setTimeout fragility; shader magic numbers PRECISION_RATIO_HIGH/LOW/GGX_EPSILON/DIR_LIGHT_DIST/MAX_DIST candidates; store init race conditions RC-1..RC-5; institutional knowledge — error handling grade summary, camera state truth ownership, bundle optimization 630KB→484KB, 2026-04-12 cleanup -319 lines (d03 summary lines 248-281).

entries.preservable_signal: Real bug analysis — needNoise defaulting to 0.5 + uVolStepJitter=1.0 means _volSeed = mix(0.5, 0.5, 1.0) = 0.5 → gate hash depends only on d during nav → whole screen fires/skips together → bands. Lesson preserved verbatim: 'any feature whose body relies on stochasticSeed for spatial decorrelation needs a corresponding needNoise clause in ray.ts. The default 0.5 value is a footgun.' 'Didn't pan out' table (loop-invariant hoists neutral; stronger gate-hash mixing 2.4× slower; per-frame phase shift breaks design rule; B-set audit items reverted). Bench-harness extension flags. Open/deferred items (Beer-Lambert attenuation on shadow rays; unified σ with atmosphere fog) (d09 summary lines 68-70).
